name: RFM Analysis
questions: Which customers are most valuable by recency, frequency, and monetary value?
Which high-frequency customers have stopped buying recently (lapsed VIPs)?
What is the revenue concentration across RFM tiers?
techniques: Quartile-based RFM scoring (R/F/M each scored 1-4)
K-means clustering on RFM scores to segment customers
Cohort comparison of RFM tiers by acquisition period

name: Cohort Retention Analysis
questions: How does retention differ across acquisition cohorts?
At which time period does the sharpest churn occur?
Which cohort has the highest 90-day retention rate?
techniques: Cohort table: retention rate by weeks/months since acquisition
Rolling retention curve with confidence bands
Survival analysis on churn events

name: Funnel Analysis
questions: At which funnel stage is drop-off highest?
How does conversion rate vary by user segment or channel?
What is the time-to-convert distribution at each stage?
techniques: Ordered stage completion rates with waterfall chart
Segment breakdown of conversion at each stage
Time-between-stages distribution analysis

name: Pareto / ABC Analysis
questions: What percentage of products account for 80% of revenue?
Which items/customers should be in tier A vs B vs C?
Is there dangerous revenue concentration in a few products?
techniques: Cumulative revenue curve by item sorted descending
ABC classification: A (top 80%), B (next 15%), C (bottom 5%)
Gini coefficient for concentration measurement

name: Customer Lifetime Value (CLV)
questions: What is the average and median customer lifetime value?
How does CLV differ across acquisition channels or segments?
Which customer attributes predict high lifetime value?
techniques: Historical CLV: sum of all transactions per customer
Predictive CLV using average order value × purchase frequency × retention period
CLV distribution with percentile breakdown

name: Churn Prediction Analysis
questions: What behavioral signals precede customer churn?
Is churn rate accelerating over time?
Which segment has the highest churn rate?
techniques: Churn rate by time period and segment
Pre-churn behavior pattern analysis (activity decay)
Logistic regression feature importance for churn drivers

name: Revenue Decomposition
questions: How much of revenue growth comes from volume vs price changes?
Which product line or segment drives the most revenue growth?
What is the revenue bridge from last period to this period?
techniques: Volume-price-mix decomposition of revenue delta
Waterfall chart: new vs expansion vs contraction vs churn revenue
Rolling 3-month revenue growth attribution by segment

name: A/B Test Significance Analysis
questions: Is the observed difference between variants statistically significant?
What is the minimum detectable effect given the sample size?
Is there a novelty effect biasing early results?
techniques: Two-proportion z-test for conversion rate difference
Mann-Whitney U test for non-normal metric distributions
Sequential testing to avoid peeking problems

name: Time Series Trend & Seasonality
questions: Is there a statistically significant upward or downward trend?
What is the seasonal pattern — does performance peak in specific months?
Are there calendar effects (weekday, holiday) that distort comparisons?
techniques: STL decomposition: trend + seasonal + residual components
Year-over-year and month-over-month growth rates
Rolling average smoothing to separate signal from noise

name: Anomaly & Outlier Detection
questions: Which records are statistical outliers that warrant investigation?
Is there a temporal cluster of anomalies suggesting a systemic issue?
What attributes distinguish anomalous records from normal ones?
techniques: IQR-based outlier flagging per column
Isolation Forest for multivariate anomaly scoring
Z-score threshold (|z| > 3) detection with contextual explanation

name: Market Basket / Association Rules
questions: Which products are most frequently purchased together?
What are the highest-lift product associations?
Are there items that cannibalize each other's sales?
techniques: Apriori algorithm for frequent itemsets (min_support=0.02)
Lift and confidence metrics for association rules
Co-purchase heatmap by product category

name: Segmentation / Clustering
questions: How many natural customer/user segments exist in the data?
What are the defining characteristics of each segment?
Which segment is most valuable or highest risk?
techniques: K-means clustering with elbow method for k selection
Silhouette score to evaluate cluster quality
PCA for dimensionality reduction before clustering

name: Geographic / Regional Analysis
questions: Which regions are over- or under-performing relative to their size?
Is there geographic clustering of high-value customers?
What is the penetration rate by market area?
techniques: Regional performance index (actual vs expected by population)
Location-based concentration analysis
Region-to-region growth rate comparison

name: Price Elasticity Analysis
questions: How sensitive is demand to price changes?
At what price point does demand drop sharply?
Does discounting cannibalize full-price purchases?
techniques: Log-log regression: ln(quantity) ~ ln(price) for elasticity coefficient
Price band analysis: volume and revenue by price tier
Promotion lift analysis: comparing periods with/without discounts

name: Workforce / HR Analytics
questions: Which departments or roles have the highest attrition rates?
Is there a salary band below which attrition spikes?
How does tenure distribution vary by department?
techniques: Attrition rate by department, tenure band, and compensation quartile
Survival analysis for time-to-voluntary-exit
Compensation equity analysis: pay gap by group controlling for role/level

name: Inventory Turnover & Demand Forecasting
questions: Which SKUs have critically low or high inventory relative to demand?
What is the average days-of-stock-on-hand by product category?
Are there recurring stockout patterns for high-velocity items?
techniques: Inventory turnover ratio: COGS / average inventory value
Days of inventory outstanding (DIO) by SKU
Demand volatility coefficient of variation per item

name: Net Promoter Score (NPS) Analysis
questions: What is the NPS distribution and how does it differ by segment?
Which customer attributes predict promoters vs detractors?
Is NPS trending up or down over time?
techniques: NPS segmentation: promoters (9-10), passives (7-8), detractors (0-6)
NPS by customer segment, product version, or support tier
Correlation between NPS and retention/expansion revenue

name: Waterfall / Bridge Analysis
questions: What are the biggest drivers of the change from period A to period B?
Which line items explain the variance between actual and budget?
Is the gap driven by volume, price, or mix effects?
techniques: Waterfall decomposition: sorted contributors to total change
Variance analysis: actual vs budget vs prior period
Contribution margin bridge by product/segment

name: Correlation & Multicollinearity Analysis
questions: Which metrics are strongly correlated and might be redundant?
Is there a leading indicator that predicts the target metric?
Are any variables collinear, reducing model reliability?
techniques: Pearson/Spearman correlation matrix with significance testing
Variance Inflation Factor (VIF) for multicollinearity detection
Partial correlation controlling for confounding variables

name: Benchmarking & Percentile Ranking
questions: Which entities are in the top and bottom quartile of performance?
How does performance distribute relative to targets or benchmarks?
What separates top-decile performers from the median?
techniques: Percentile ranking of all entities on key metrics
Distribution comparison: actual vs benchmark using KDE plot
Top-decile vs bottom-decile feature contrast analysis

name: Fraud & Data Integrity Detection
questions: Are there duplicate transactions that suggest data quality issues or fraud?
Do any amounts follow Benford's Law violations (a fraud signal)?
Are there patterns of transactions just below approval thresholds?
techniques: Benford's Law test on transaction amounts
Round-number clustering: how many amounts end in .00 or .99?
Duplicate detection: exact and near-duplicate transaction matching

name: Campaign / Channel Attribution
questions: Which acquisition channel has the lowest cost-per-acquisition?
What is the revenue ROI for each marketing campaign?
Is there diminishing return on spend above a certain threshold?
techniques: CAC and ROI by channel and campaign
Spend efficiency curve: revenue per dollar by channel
Time-lag analysis: conversion window by channel

name: Quality Control / SPC Analysis
questions: Is the process in statistical control or are there out-of-control signals?
What percentage of output is within specification limits?
Are defect rates trending upward over time?
techniques: Control chart (X-bar and R chart) for process stability
Process capability index (Cpk): how centered is output within spec?
Defect Pareto: which defect types account for 80% of failures?

name: Feature Adoption & Engagement Analysis
questions: Which features have low adoption despite high business value?
Is there a usage pattern that distinguishes retained vs churned users?
What is the time-to-first-use for key features after signup?
techniques: Feature adoption rate: % of active users who use each feature
Usage frequency distribution by feature
Correlation between feature adoption breadth and retention

name: Lead Scoring & Pipeline Analysis
questions: What is the win rate and average deal size by sales stage?
Which lead sources produce the highest quality pipeline?
Where in the funnel is the most revenue being lost?
techniques: Win rate by stage, lead source, and deal size bucket
Average days-in-stage and stage velocity analysis
Pipeline coverage ratio: total pipeline vs quota

name: Patient / Clinical Outcome Analysis
questions: What is the readmission rate within 30 days by diagnosis group?
Are there demographic factors associated with worse outcomes?
Which treatment protocols have the highest success rates?
techniques: 30/60/90-day readmission rate by diagnosis and demographics
Odds ratio analysis for outcome risk factors
Length-of-stay distribution by diagnosis category

name: Logistics & Delivery Performance
questions: What percentage of deliveries arrive on time, and how does it vary by carrier?
What is the average and P95 transit time by route?
Are delays concentrated in specific warehouses or regions?
techniques: On-time delivery rate by carrier, region, and product category
Transit time distribution with P50/P90/P99 breakdown
Delay root cause attribution by warehouse and shipping lane

name: Subscription MRR / ARR Analysis
questions: What is the MRR breakdown: new, expansion, contraction, churn?
What is the net revenue retention (NRR) rate?
Which plan tier has the highest expansion rate?
techniques: MRR waterfall: new + expansion - contraction - churn
Net Revenue Retention: (MRR at end + expansion - contraction - churn) / MRR at start
Plan upgrade/downgrade transition matrix

name: Survey Response & Text Sentiment Analysis
questions: What are the most common themes in open-ended responses?
Is sentiment different across customer segments or time periods?
What topics correlate with low satisfaction scores?
techniques: Word frequency and bigram analysis of open-ended text
Sentiment polarity scoring (positive/negative/neutral)
Topic clustering via TF-IDF and k-means

name: Cost Structure & Margin Analysis
questions: Which product lines or segments have the thinnest gross margins?
How has gross margin trended over time, and what drives changes?
Are there fixed costs that could be optimized given revenue level?
techniques: Gross margin % by product, segment, and period
Cost decomposition: variable vs fixed vs semi-variable
Break-even analysis: revenue needed to cover fixed costs

name: Competitive Benchmarking
questions: How does our performance compare to peers on key metrics?
Are we gaining or losing market share relative to competitors?
On which dimensions are we a clear leader or laggard?
techniques: Spider/radar chart comparison across key dimensions
Market share trend analysis by period
Index comparison: our score as % of best-in-class
